articles.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission: India Accelerates Towards Production Goals
articles.explainer:
• India's ambitious National Green Hydrogen Mission is reportedly on track, with significant progress in policy framework and initial project allocations.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization.
• Recent policy announcements include incentives for electrolyser manufacturing and a framework for green hydrogen demand creation in key sectors like refining and fertilizers.
• This initiative is crucial for India to meet its net-zero emissions target by 2070 and reduce reliance on fossil fuel imports.
• Public-private partnerships are being encouraged to scale up production and reduce the cost of green hydrogen.
gsSubTopics: Green Hydrogen Mission
renewable energy
energy security
climate change mitigation
industrial growth
technological innovation
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy / Economic Times
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in 2023.
Target to produce 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030.
SIGHT (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) Programme is a key component.
Role of Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE).
Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the economic potential of green hydrogen as an export commodity and its impact on industrial sectors.
Examine the challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production, including technology costs, infrastructure, and storage.
Evaluate the role of government policies and international collaborations in achieving the mission's objectives.
Compare green hydrogen with other clean energy sources in the context of India's energy mix.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production facility with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Environment
prelimsSyllabus.area: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment

articles.headline: Kolkata Rape and Murder Case: Convict Sentenced to Rigorous Life Imprisonment
articles.explainer:
• The Sealdah Court in Kolkata has sentenced Sanjay Roy to rigorous life imprisonment for the brutal 2024 Kolkata rape and murder case.
• The verdict follows a swift trial, highlighting the judiciary's commitment to delivering justice in heinous crimes against women.
• The case had sparked widespread outrage and calls for stricter laws and faster judicial processes for crimes against women.
• This judgment reinforces the importance of forensic evidence and robust investigative procedures in securing convictions.
• Legal experts note the sentencing serves as a deterrent and a step towards ensuring women's safety.
gsSubTopics: mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of vulnerable sections
judiciary
women's safety
prelimsPoints: Indian Penal Code (IPC) sections related to rape and murder (e.g., 376, 302).
Role of fast-track courts in expediting trials for heinous crimes.
National Crime Records Bureau (NCRB) data on crimes against women.
mainsPoints: Analyze the effectiveness of existing laws and judicial mechanisms in addressing crimes against women in India.
Discuss the societal implications of such verdicts in promoting women's safety and gender equality.
Suggest reforms needed in the criminal justice system to ensure faster trials and convictions in cases of sexual violence.
Examine the role of public awareness and civil society organizations in advocating for justice and systemic change.
Evaluate the challenges faced by victims and their families in navigating the legal process.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel and law books on a wooden desk, symbolizing justice.
articles.trivia: The 'Nirbhaya Fund' was established in India in 2013 by the Ministry of Finance to support initiatives for women's safety.

articles.headline: Maharashtra Train Accident: 13 Killed, Railway Safety Under Intense Scrutiny
articles.explainer:
• Thirteen people were killed and five others injured after a passenger train collided with a group of people near Pachora, Maharashtra.
• The victims had disembarked from another train due to a false fire alarm and were on the tracks when the collision occurred.
• The incident has raised serious questions about railway safety protocols, crowd management, and emergency response mechanisms.
• Railway authorities have initiated a high-level inquiry to determine the cause and accountability for the tragic loss of life.
• Experts are calling for enhanced public awareness campaigns regarding railway safety and stricter enforcement of rules.
gsSubTopics: disaster and disaster management
infrastructure (railways)
internal security challenges
prelimsPoints: Indian Railways is the fourth-largest railway network in the world.
Railways Act, 1989 governs railway operations in India.
Role of Commissioner of Railway Safety (CRS) for accident inquiries.
Kavach system (Automatic Train Protection) is being implemented to prevent collisions.
mainsPoints: Analyze the systemic issues contributing to railway accidents in India, including infrastructure, human error, and safety protocols.
Discuss the measures that can be adopted to enhance railway safety, focusing on technology, public awareness, and emergency response.
Evaluate the challenges in managing large crowds at railway stations and during emergencies.
Examine the role of regulatory bodies and government policies in ensuring safe railway operations.
Suggest ways to improve coordination between different agencies during railway accidents and disaster relief operations.
articles.imageDescription: Rescue workers near train tracks after an accident, with emergency vehicles.
articles.trivia: The first passenger train in India ran on April 16, 1853, from Bori Bunder (Mumbai) to Thane.

articles.headline: Karnataka Highway Tragedy: 10 Dead in Truck Accident, Road Safety Under Scrutiny
articles.explainer:
• A tragic accident occurred in Yellapur, Karnataka, where a truck fell into a ravine along National Highway 63, killing 10 people and injuring 15 others.
• The incident has brought the critical issue of road safety, particularly on national highways and ghat sections, back into focus.
• Preliminary investigations suggest possible factors like driver fatigue, vehicle mechanical failure, or hazardous road conditions.
• Authorities have pledged to review road engineering, signage, and enforcement of traffic rules on accident-prone stretches.
• Public health and safety advocates are calling for comprehensive audits of commercial vehicle fitness and driver training programs.
gsSubTopics: disaster and disaster management
governance
issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways (MoRTH) is responsible for national highways.
Motor Vehicles Act, 1988 (amended 2019) governs road transport.
India has one of the highest road accident fatalities globally.
Good Samaritan Law protects bystanders helping accident victims.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multi-faceted causes of road accidents in India, including human factors, vehicle defects, and environmental conditions.
Discuss government initiatives and policies aimed at improving road safety, such as engineering improvements, enforcement, and awareness campaigns.
Evaluate the role of technology, like Intelligent Transport Systems (ITS), in preventing accidents and enhancing emergency response.
Examine the economic and social burden of road accidents on individuals and the nation.
Suggest comprehensive strategies for achieving a significant reduction in road accident fatalities and injuries.
articles.imageDescription: A damaged truck at the bottom of a steep ravine on a winding mountain road.
articles.trivia: India accounts for about 11% of the total road accident deaths in the world, despite having only 1% of the world's vehicles.

articles.headline: Veteran Politician Shamsheer Singh Manhas Passes Away at 65
articles.explainer:
• Shamsheer Singh Manhas, a prominent politician and former Member of Parliament, passed away at the age of 65.
• Manhas had a long and distinguished career in public service, representing his constituents with dedication.
• He was known for his contributions to regional development and his advocacy for socio-economic issues in his state.
• Political leaders across the spectrum expressed their condolences, remembering him as a committed public servant.
• His demise marks the end of an era for a significant political voice in the region.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
prelimsPoints: Role and functions of a Member of Parliament (MP).
Process of election to Rajya Sabha (if he was an RS MP).
Constitutional provisions related to the Parliament of India.
Importance of regional political leaders in a diverse democracy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of seasoned politicians in shaping regional and national policy.
Analyze the challenges faced by public representatives in balancing regional interests with national objectives.
Evaluate the impact of individual political personalities on democratic processes and governance.
Examine the contributions of veteran leaders to institution building and political discourse.
Reflect on the importance of public service and ethical leadership in a democratic setup.
articles.imageDescription: A formal portrait of Shamsheer Singh Manhas.
articles.trivia: The minimum age to become a Member of Parliament (Lok Sabha) is 25 years, and for Rajya Sabha, it is 30 years.

articles.headline: RBI Releases Quarterly Inflation Report: Focus on Food Prices and Global Volatility
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has released its latest quarterly inflation report, highlighting persistent concerns over food price inflation.
• The report indicates that global supply chain disruptions and geopolitical tensions continue to contribute to inflationary pressures.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to maintaining price stability while supporting economic growth, signaling a cautious approach to monetary policy.
• Analysts suggest that the central bank might hold interest rates steady in the near term, awaiting clearer trends in inflation and economic activity.
• The report also emphasizes the need for structural reforms to address supply-side bottlenecks and improve agricultural productivity.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment
inflation
monetary policy
prelimsPoints: RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) sets the policy interest rate (repo rate) to achieve the inflation target.
Inflation target for CPI is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
Tools of monetary policy include repo rate, reverse repo rate, CRR, SLR, OMOs.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to current inflationary trends in India, distinguishing between demand-pull and cost-push inflation.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with economic growth objectives.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in managing inflation in the Indian context.
Examine the impact of global economic volatility and geopolitical events on India's inflation outlook.
Suggest fiscal and supply-side measures that the government can implement to complement RBI's efforts in managing inflation.
articles.imageDescription: A chart showing inflation trends with upward arrows, overlaid with the RBI logo.
articles.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

articles.headline: India-France Strategic Dialogue Concludes: Deepening Defence and Space Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-France Strategic Dialogue concluded today, reaffirming the strong bilateral partnership between the two nations.
• Discussions focused on enhancing cooperation in defence, space technology, cybersecurity, and counter-terrorism.
• Both sides emphasized their shared vision for a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific and committed to working together on regional security issues.
• Key outcomes included agreements on joint research and development in defence manufacturing and greater collaboration in satellite technology.
• The dialogue underscored the growing convergence of strategic interests between India and France.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
articles.source: Ministry of External Affairs / The Indian Express
prelimsPoints: India and France established a Strategic Partnership in 1998.
France is a key supplier of defence equipment to India (e.g., Rafale jets, Scorpene submarines).
Both countries are partners in the International Solar Alliance (ISA).
India and France conduct joint military exercises like Varuna (Navy), Garuda (Air Force), and Shakti (Army).
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution and significance of the India-France Strategic Partnership in the context of changing global geopolitics.
Discuss the key pillars of cooperation between India and France, particularly in defence, space, and nuclear energy.
Evaluate the role of India and France in promoting stability and security in the Indo-Pacific region.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for further deepening bilateral ties, including economic and technological collaboration.
Compare India's strategic relations with France to those with other major global powers.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and France displayed together during a diplomatic meeting.
articles.trivia: The first Indian astronaut, Rakesh Sharma, flew aboard a Soviet spacecraft, Soyuz T-11, in 1984, which included a French cosmonaut.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology Demonstrator
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has successfully conducted a critical test flight of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator.
• This achievement marks a significant step towards developing a fully reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle, reducing launch costs.
• The test involved a controlled landing experiment, simulating the return of a space vehicle to Earth.
• Reusable launch vehicles are crucial for future space exploration and commercial space missions, offering economic and environmental benefits.
• ISRO aims to master this technology to enhance India's competitiveness in the global space market.
gsSubTopics: achievements of Indians in science & technology
indigenization of technology and developing new technology
space technology
prelimsPoints: RLV-TD is India's first step towards developing a reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle.
It aims to reduce the cost of space access significantly.
Key experiments include Hypersonic Flight Experiment (HEX), Landing Experiment (LEX), Return Flight Experiment (REX), Scramjet Propulsion Experiment (SPEX).
ISRO is headquartered in Bengaluru.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's space program and its global standing.
Analyze the economic and environmental benefits of developing reusable launch vehicles.
Examine the challenges involved in mastering RLV technology, including materials science, aerodynamics, and propulsion systems.
Evaluate ISRO's trajectory in space technology development and its contribution to 'Atmanirbhar Bharat'.
Suggest how India can leverage RLV technology to further commercialize its space sector.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) descending for a landing.
articles.trivia: ISRO holds the record for launching 104 satellites in a single mission in February 2017, using the PSLV-C37.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Environmental Clearance for Key Infrastructure Project with Stricter Conditions
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court has upheld the environmental clearance granted to a major infrastructure project, but imposed stricter conditions for its implementation.
• The ruling emphasizes the judiciary's role in balancing economic development with environmental protection and sustainable practices.
• The project, critical for regional connectivity and economic growth, had faced challenges from environmental activists over potential ecological impact.
• The new conditions include enhanced compensatory afforestation, robust waste management plans, and regular environmental impact assessments.
• This judgment sets a precedent for future large-scale projects, mandating higher environmental accountability.
gsSubTopics: judiciary
government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) is mandatory for certain projects under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.
National Green Tribunal (NGT) deals with environmental cases.
Concept of 'polluter pays principle' and 'precautionary principle'.
Role of Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC).
mainsPoints: Discuss the intricate balance between economic development and environmental conservation in the context of India's growth trajectory.
Analyze the role of the judiciary, particularly the Supreme Court, in environmental governance and policy implementation.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) process in India and suggest necessary reforms.
Examine the concept of 'sustainable development' and how it is being integrated into infrastructure planning.
Discuss the challenges in ensuring compliance with environmental conditions for large-scale projects.
articles.imageDescription: A highway passing through a green landscape, with a symbol of environmental protection superimposed.
articles.trivia: India was the first country to make environmental protection a constitutional mandate (Article 48A and 51A(g)).

articles.headline: Government Launches 'PM-SURAJ' Scheme for Skill Development in Rural Areas
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government has officially launched the 'Pradhan Mantri – Samagra Unnayan aur Rozgar Abhiyan (PM-SURAJ)' scheme.
• The scheme aims to boost skill development and provide employment opportunities for rural youth and marginalized communities.
• It integrates existing skill development programs and introduces new initiatives focusing on demand-driven vocational training.
• PM-SURAJ emphasizes entrepreneurship development and access to credit for setting up small enterprises in rural India.
• The initiative is expected to play a crucial role in reducing rural unemployment and fostering inclusive growth.
gsSubTopics: government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Human Resources
prelimsPoints: PM-SURAJ is an umbrella scheme consolidating various skill development initiatives.
Focus on rural areas and vulnerable sections (SC, ST, OBC, EWS, sanitation workers).
Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment is likely the nodal ministry.
Other related schemes: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY), DDU-GKY.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of skill development in addressing rural unemployment and poverty in India.
Analyze the potential impact of the PM-SURAJ scheme on inclusive growth and social empowerment of marginalized communities.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing skill development programs in rural areas, including infrastructure, trainer availability, and market linkages.
Examine the role of public-private partnerships and digital platforms in enhancing the reach and effectiveness of such schemes.
Suggest measures to ensure that skill development initiatives are aligned with industry demands and future job market trends.
articles.imageDescription: A group of young men and women learning vocational skills in a rural setting.
articles.trivia: India has the world's largest youth population, presenting both a demographic dividend and a challenge for skill development.

articles.headline: New Guidelines for Data Privacy in Healthcare Sector Released by Ministry of Health
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare has released comprehensive new guidelines aimed at strengthening data privacy and security in the healthcare sector.
• The guidelines address the collection, storage, sharing, and usage of sensitive personal health information (SPHI) by healthcare providers and digital platforms.
• They mandate strict consent mechanisms, data anonymization, and robust cybersecurity measures to protect patient confidentiality.
• The move comes in response to the increasing digitization of health records and concerns over potential data breaches and misuse.
• Experts commend the initiative as a crucial step towards building trust in digital health services and aligning with global data protection standards.
gsSubTopics: governance
issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health
e-governance
cyber security
articles.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare / The Hindu BusinessLine
prelimsPoints: National Digital Health Mission (NDHM) aims to create a digital health ecosystem.
Personal Data Protection Bill, 2019 (expected to be enacted as law).
Right to Privacy recognized as a fundamental right (Puttaswamy judgment).
Role of Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) and Ministry of Health.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges and opportunities presented by the digitization of health records in India.
Analyze the importance of robust data privacy frameworks in building trust in digital health services and protecting patient rights.
Evaluate the key provisions of the new guidelines and their potential impact on healthcare providers and patients.
Examine the ethical considerations involved in handling sensitive personal health information.
Suggest measures to ensure effective implementation and enforcement of data privacy regulations in the healthcare sector.
articles.imageDescription: A padlock icon over a medical record, symbolizing data security in healthcare.
articles.trivia: The 'Aarogya Setu' app, launched during the COVID-19 pandemic, was one of India's largest digital health initiatives.

articles.headline: India's Renewable Energy Capacity Crosses 200 GW Mark: A Milestone Achievement
articles.explainer:
• India has achieved a significant milestone, with its installed renewable energy capacity (excluding large hydro) now exceeding 200 Gigawatts (GW).
• This achievement underscores India's rapid progress in its energy transition and commitment to climate goals.
• Solar and wind power constitute the largest share of this capacity, driven by favorable government policies and decreasing technology costs.
• The milestone brings India closer to its target of 500 GW non-fossil fuel capacity by 2030.
• This expansion is crucial for enhancing energy security, reducing carbon emissions, and attracting green investments.
gsSubTopics: conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
infrastructure: energy
science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: India's target: 500 GW non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
International Solar Alliance (ISA) is an Indian initiative.
Government schemes: PM-KUSUM, Solar Parks Scheme.
Major renewable energy sources: Solar, Wind, Biomass, Small Hydro.
mainsPoints: Discuss the factors that have contributed to India's rapid growth in renewable energy capacity.
Analyze the economic and environmental benefits of transitioning to a renewable energy-dominated power sector.
Examine the challenges in integrating high shares of intermittent renewable energy into the national grid.
Evaluate the government's policies and initiatives aimed at promoting renewable energy, including financial incentives and regulatory frameworks.
Suggest future strategies for India to achieve its ambitious renewable energy targets and ensure energy security.
articles.imageDescription: A vast solar power plant stretching to the horizon under a clear sky.
articles.trivia: The world's largest solar park, Bhadla Solar Park, is located in Rajasthan, India.

articles.headline: Challenges in Urban Planning: Report Highlights Gaps in Smart Cities Mission Implementation
articles.explainer:
• A recent report by an independent think tank highlights significant implementation gaps and challenges within India's Smart Cities Mission.
• The report points to issues such as inadequate public participation, funding bottlenecks, and lack of inter-agency coordination.
• While some cities have shown commendable progress, others struggle with project delays and failure to achieve desired outcomes in areas like urban mobility and waste management.
• The analysis suggests a need for re-evaluating the mission's strategy, focusing on capacity building at the municipal level and decentralized planning.
• Urban development experts advocate for a more inclusive and sustainable approach to smart city development.
gsSubTopics: government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
urbanization, their problems and their remedies
articles.source: Centre for Policy Research / The Economic Times
prelimsPoints: Smart Cities Mission launched in 2015.
Aims to promote cities that provide core infrastructure, a clean and sustainable environment and application of 'Smart' solutions.
100 cities selected under the mission.
Other urban schemes: AMRUT, HRIDAY.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the performance and achievements of the Smart Cities Mission since its inception.
Discuss the major challenges in urban planning and governance that hinder the effective implementation of smart city projects.
Analyze the role of technology and citizen engagement in making urban areas more livable and sustainable.
Suggest policy reforms and strategic interventions to address the identified gaps in the Smart Cities Mission.
Examine the concept of 'inclusive urbanization' and how it can be integrated into future urban development plans.
articles.imageDescription: An aerial view of a modern city skyline with digital overlays representing smart city concepts.
articles.trivia: India's urban population is projected to reach 600 million by 2030, making urban planning a critical area.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery Remains in Bihar
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has announced the discovery of significant remains of an ancient Buddhist monastery in Bihar.
• The excavation revealed monastic cells, prayer halls, and artifacts dating back to the Pala period (8th-12th centuries CE).
• This discovery adds to the rich Buddhist heritage of Bihar, a region historically significant for the spread of Buddhism.
• Archaeologists believe the site could provide crucial insights into the architectural styles, monastic life, and intellectual traditions of the era.
• The ASI plans further excavations and conservation efforts to preserve the newly found heritage site.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
ancient history
articles.source: Archaeological Survey of India / The Economic Times
prelimsPoints: Bihar is home to major Buddhist sites like Bodh Gaya, Nalanda, Vikramshila, and Rajgir.
Pala Dynasty ruled parts of Bengal and Bihar from 8th to 12th century CE.
ASI is under the Ministry of Culture.
Important Buddhist architectural elements: Stupas, Viharas, Chaityas.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Buddhist monastic sites in understanding ancient Indian history, art, and culture.
Analyze the contributions of the Pala dynasty to the development of Buddhist art and architecture.
Examine the challenges and importance of archaeological excavations and conservation in preserving India's cultural heritage.
Evaluate the role of such discoveries in promoting cultural tourism and regional development.
Suggest measures for better protection and management of newly discovered archaeological sites.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists working at an excavation site, revealing ancient brick structures of a monastery.
articles.trivia: Nalanda University, an ancient center of learning in Bihar, flourished during the Gupta and Pala periods and was a major Buddhist monastic university.

articles.headline: Border Security Force Foils Major Infiltration Bid in Jammu Sector
articles.explainer:
• The Border Security Force (BSF) successfully foiled a major infiltration bid along the International Border in the Jammu sector.
• BSF troops detected and engaged a group of armed infiltrators attempting to cross into Indian territory under the cover of dense fog.
• The incident highlights the persistent security challenges faced by India along its western borders, particularly from state-sponsored terrorism.
• Security forces remain on high alert, intensifying surveillance and patrolling activities to counter such threats.
• This operation underscores the unwavering vigilance and professionalism of India's border guarding forces.
gsSubTopics: security challenges and their management in border areas
linkages of organized crime with terrorism
various security forces and agencies and their mandate
articles.source: Ministry of Home Affairs / The Economic Times
prelimsPoints: BSF is India's primary border guarding organization on the Pakistan and Bangladesh borders.
It comes under the Ministry of Home Affairs.
Line of Control (LoC) vs International Border (IB).
Role of various Central Armed Police Forces (CAPFs).
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolving nature of cross-border infiltration and its implications for India's internal security.
Discuss the multi-pronged strategy employed by Indian security forces to counter infiltration and terrorism in border areas.
Evaluate the role of technology, such as surveillance systems and anti-infiltration obstacle systems, in enhancing border security.
Examine the geopolitical factors contributing to persistent border challenges, particularly in the Jammu and Kashmir region.
Suggest measures to strengthen coordination between various security agencies for effective border management.
articles.imageDescription: BSF jawans patrolling a foggy border area with weapons.
articles.trivia: The BSF was formed in 1965 in the wake of the 1965 Indo-Pak War.

articles.headline: India Ratifies Key ILO Convention on Occupational Safety and Health
articles.explainer:
• India has ratified a crucial International Labour Organization (ILO) Convention pertaining to occupational safety and health (OSH).
• This ratification signals India's commitment to ensuring safer working conditions and promoting the welfare of its vast workforce.
• The convention sets international standards for national policies and programs on OSH, including risk assessment, prevention, and training.
• The move is expected to drive reforms in India's labor laws and enforcement mechanisms to align with global best practices.
• Labor rights advocates welcome the ratification as a significant step towards protecting workers' rights and reducing workplace accidents.
gsSubTopics: welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
India and its neighborhood- relations
bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
articles.source: Ministry of Labour & Employment / The Economic Times
prelimsPoints: ILO is a specialized agency of the United Nations.
India is a founding member of the ILO (1919).
Constitutional provisions related to labor: Concurrent List, Article 39(e), 42, 43.
Recent Labour Codes: Code on Wages, Industrial Relations Code, Code on Social Security, OSH & Working Conditions Code.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's ratification of the ILO Convention on Occupational Safety and Health for its workforce and international image.
Analyze the current state of occupational safety and health in India, highlighting major challenges across different sectors.
Evaluate the potential impact of this ratification on India's labor laws, enforcement mechanisms, and industrial practices.
Examine the role of trade unions, employers, and government in promoting a culture of safety at workplaces.
Suggest comprehensive measures to improve occupational safety and health standards, including training, technology, and regulatory oversight.
articles.imageDescription: A pair of safety gloves and a hard hat on a workbench, symbolizing workplace safety.
articles.trivia: The ILO was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize in 1969 for its efforts to improve peace among classes, pursue justice for workers, and provide technical assistance to developing nations.

highlights: India's National Green Hydrogen Mission shows significant progress, accelerating towards production and export goals.
Kolkata Rape and Murder Case: Convict sentenced to rigorous life imprisonment, reinforcing judicial commitment to women's safety.
Tragic Maharashtra Train Accident claims 13 lives, prompting intense scrutiny on railway safety protocols and emergency response.
RBI's Quarterly Inflation Report highlights persistent concerns over food prices and global volatility, signaling cautious monetary policy.
ISRO successfully tests its Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology Demonstrator, marking a major step towards cost-effective space access.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. Its primary objective is to achieve net-zero emissions by 2030.
2. The SIGHT (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) Programme is a key component of the mission.
3. Green hydrogen is produced by the electrolysis of water using renewable energy sources.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. India's net-zero emissions target is by 2070, not 2030. The mission aims to produce 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030. Statement 2 is correct. The SIGHT programme provides financial incentives for electrolyser manufacturing and green hydrogen production. Statement 3 is correct. Green hydrogen is defined by its production method, using renewable energy to power the electrolysis of water.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is primarily responsible for conducting inquiries into railway accidents in India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Commissioner of Railway Safety (CRS) is the authority responsible for statutory inquiries into serious railway accidents in India. The CRS functions under the Ministry of Civil Aviation, ensuring independence from the Ministry of Railways. NDMA is for overall disaster management, Ministry of Railways for policy and operations, and RPF for security.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding India's renewable energy capacity:
1. India's target is to achieve 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
2. The International Solar Alliance (ISA) is an initiative led by India and France.
3. Large hydro projects are typically included in the 'renewable energy capacity' figures released by the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. India has set a target of 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030. Statement 2 is correct. The ISA was jointly launched by India and France in 2015. Statement 3 is incorrect. Typically, 'renewable energy capacity' figures, especially those used for targets, often exclude large hydro projects, which are sometimes categorized separately due to their different environmental and operational characteristics compared to solar or wind.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'Puttaswamy judgment' is a landmark Supreme Court ruling in India primarily associated with which of the following?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The K.S. Puttaswamy (Retd.) vs Union of India judgment in 2017 declared the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution. This judgment has significant implications for data protection and individual liberties in the digital age.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a Central Armed Police Force (CAPF) under the Ministry of Home Affairs?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Indian Coast Guard (ICG) is a maritime law enforcement and search and rescue agency of India. It operates under the Ministry of Defence. BSF, CRPF, and NSG are all Central Armed Police Forces (CAPFs) that function under the Ministry of Home Affairs.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze the progress and challenges of India's National Green Hydrogen Mission. What strategic interventions are necessary to position India as a global leader in green hydrogen production and export?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; Infrastructure: energy; Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the mission's objectives and its significance for India. Discuss the achievements and policy frameworks in place. Analyze key challenges such as technology cost, infrastructure development, storage, and demand creation. Suggest strategic interventions including R&D, policy incentives, international collaborations, and skill development to overcome these challenges and achieve global leadership.

mainsMocks.question: The recent train accident in Maharashtra highlights persistent concerns regarding railway safety in India. Discuss the systemic issues contributing to such accidents and suggest comprehensive measures, including technological advancements and policy reforms, to enhance railway safety and prevent future tragedies.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Disaster and disaster management; Infrastructure (railways); GS2: Governance.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by acknowledging the gravity of railway accidents and their impact. Identify systemic issues like aging infrastructure, human error, inadequate safety protocols, and crowd management. Propose multi-pronged solutions including implementation of advanced safety technologies (e.g., Kavach), improved maintenance, public awareness campaigns, regulatory strengthening, and enhanced emergency response mechanisms.

mainsMocks.question: Despite significant investments and policy focus, urban planning and the implementation of the Smart Cities Mission face numerous challenges in India. Critically evaluate these challenges and suggest a more inclusive and sustainable approach to urban development in the country.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation; Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the Smart Cities Mission and its objectives. Discuss the challenges such as funding bottlenecks, lack of public participation, inter-agency coordination issues, and uneven progress. Suggest a more inclusive approach emphasizing decentralized planning, capacity building at municipal levels, community engagement, environmental sustainability, and leveraging technology for citizen-centric services rather than just infrastructure.

quickBytes.text: The Sealdah Court is a district court located in Kolkata, West Bengal.
National Highway 63 connects Ankola in Karnataka to Jagdalpur in Chhattisgarh.
The International Labour Organization (ILO) is a specialized agency of the United Nations.
The Pala Empire was a Buddhist dynasty that ruled from the 8th to 12th century CE in Bengal and Bihar.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of the RBI has six members: three from RBI and three external members.
ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program aims to significantly reduce the cost of space launches.
The Environment (Protection) Act, 1986 is a key legislation for environmental protection in India.
The term 'net-zero emissions' refers to achieving a balance between the amount of greenhouse gas produced and the amount removed from the atmosphere.
The Border Security Force (BSF) is one of the seven Central Armed Police Forces (CAPFs) of India.
The Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) is a flagship scheme of the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery Remains in Bihar
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has announced the discovery of significant remains of an ancient Buddhist monastery in Bihar.
• The excavation revealed monastic cells, prayer halls, and artifacts dating back to the Pala period (8th-12th centuries CE).
• This discovery adds to the rich Buddhist heritage of Bihar, a region historically significant for the spread of Buddhism.
• Archaeologists believe the site could provide crucial insights into the architectural styles, monastic life, and intellectual traditions of the era.
• The ASI plans further excavations and conservation efforts to preserve the newly found heritage site.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
ancient history
GS1.source: Archaeological Survey of India / The Economic Times
prelimsPoints: Bihar is home to major Buddhist sites like Bodh Gaya, Nalanda, Vikramshila, and Rajgir.
Pala Dynasty ruled parts of Bengal and Bihar from 8th to 12th century CE.
ASI is under the Ministry of Culture.
Important Buddhist architectural elements: Stupas, Viharas, Chaityas.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Buddhist monastic sites in understanding ancient Indian history, art, and culture.
Analyze the contributions of the Pala dynasty to the development of Buddhist art and architecture.
Examine the challenges and importance of archaeological excavations and conservation in preserving India's cultural heritage.
Evaluate the role of such discoveries in promoting cultural tourism and regional development.
Suggest measures for better protection and management of newly discovered archaeological sites.
GS1.imageDescription: Archaeologists working at an excavation site, revealing ancient brick structures of a monastery.
GS1.trivia: Nalanda University, an ancient center of learning in Bihar, flourished during the Gupta and Pala periods and was a major Buddhist monastic university.

GS2.headline: Kolkata Rape and Murder Case: Convict Sentenced to Rigorous Life Imprisonment
GS2.explainer:
• The Sealdah Court in Kolkata has sentenced Sanjay Roy to rigorous life imprisonment for the brutal 2024 Kolkata rape and murder case.
• The verdict follows a swift trial, highlighting the judiciary's commitment to delivering justice in heinous crimes against women.
• The case had sparked widespread outrage and calls for stricter laws and faster judicial processes for crimes against women.
• This judgment reinforces the importance of forensic evidence and robust investigative procedures in securing convictions.
• Legal experts note the sentencing serves as a deterrent and a step towards ensuring women's safety.
gsSubTopics: mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of vulnerable sections
judiciary
women's safety
prelimsPoints: Indian Penal Code (IPC) sections related to rape and murder (e.g., 376, 302).
Role of fast-track courts in expediting trials for heinous crimes.
National Crime Records Bureau (NCRB) data on crimes against women.
mainsPoints: Analyze the effectiveness of existing laws and judicial mechanisms in addressing crimes against women in India.
Discuss the societal implications of such verdicts in promoting women's safety and gender equality.
Suggest reforms needed in the criminal justice system to ensure faster trials and convictions in cases of sexual violence.
Examine the role of public awareness and civil society organizations in advocating for justice and systemic change.
Evaluate the challenges faced by victims and their families in navigating the legal process.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel and law books on a wooden desk, symbolizing justice.
GS2.trivia: The 'Nirbhaya Fund' was established in India in 2013 by the Ministry of Finance to support initiatives for women's safety.

GS2.headline: Karnataka Highway Tragedy: 10 Dead in Truck Accident, Road Safety Under Scrutiny
GS2.explainer:
• A tragic accident occurred in Yellapur, Karnataka, where a truck fell into a ravine along National Highway 63, killing 10 people and injuring 15 others.
• The incident has brought the critical issue of road safety, particularly on national highways and ghat sections, back into focus.
• Preliminary investigations suggest possible factors like driver fatigue, vehicle mechanical failure, or hazardous road conditions.
• Authorities have pledged to review road engineering, signage, and enforcement of traffic rules on accident-prone stretches.
• Public health and safety advocates are calling for comprehensive audits of commercial vehicle fitness and driver training programs.
gsSubTopics: disaster and disaster management
governance
issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways (MoRTH) is responsible for national highways.
Motor Vehicles Act, 1988 (amended 2019) governs road transport.
India has one of the highest road accident fatalities globally.
Good Samaritan Law protects bystanders helping accident victims.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multi-faceted causes of road accidents in India, including human factors, vehicle defects, and environmental conditions.
Discuss government initiatives and policies aimed at improving road safety, such as engineering improvements, enforcement, and awareness campaigns.
Evaluate the role of technology, like Intelligent Transport Systems (ITS), in preventing accidents and enhancing emergency response.
Examine the economic and social burden of road accidents on individuals and the nation.
Suggest comprehensive strategies for achieving a significant reduction in road accident fatalities and injuries.
GS2.imageDescription: A damaged truck at the bottom of a steep ravine on a winding mountain road.
GS2.trivia: India accounts for about 11% of the total road accident deaths in the world, despite having only 1% of the world's vehicles.

GS2.headline: Veteran Politician Shamsheer Singh Manhas Passes Away at 65
GS2.explainer:
• Shamsheer Singh Manhas, a prominent politician and former Member of Parliament, passed away at the age of 65.
• Manhas had a long and distinguished career in public service, representing his constituents with dedication.
• He was known for his contributions to regional development and his advocacy for socio-economic issues in his state.
• Political leaders across the spectrum expressed their condolences, remembering him as a committed public servant.
• His demise marks the end of an era for a significant political voice in the region.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
prelimsPoints: Role and functions of a Member of Parliament (MP).
Process of election to Rajya Sabha (if he was an RS MP).
Constitutional provisions related to the Parliament of India.
Importance of regional political leaders in a diverse democracy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of seasoned politicians in shaping regional and national policy.
Analyze the challenges faced by public representatives in balancing regional interests with national objectives.
Evaluate the impact of individual political personalities on democratic processes and governance.
Examine the contributions of veteran leaders to institution building and political discourse.
Reflect on the importance of public service and ethical leadership in a democratic setup.
GS2.imageDescription: A formal portrait of Shamsheer Singh Manhas.
GS2.trivia: The minimum age to become a Member of Parliament (Lok Sabha) is 25 years, and for Rajya Sabha, it is 30 years.

GS2.headline: India-France Strategic Dialogue Concludes: Deepening Defence and Space Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-France Strategic Dialogue concluded today, reaffirming the strong bilateral partnership between the two nations.
• Discussions focused on enhancing cooperation in defence, space technology, cybersecurity, and counter-terrorism.
• Both sides emphasized their shared vision for a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific and committed to working together on regional security issues.
• Key outcomes included agreements on joint research and development in defence manufacturing and greater collaboration in satellite technology.
• The dialogue underscored the growing convergence of strategic interests between India and France.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
GS2.source: Ministry of External Affairs / The Indian Express
prelimsPoints: India and France established a Strategic Partnership in 1998.
France is a key supplier of defence equipment to India (e.g., Rafale jets, Scorpene submarines).
Both countries are partners in the International Solar Alliance (ISA).
India and France conduct joint military exercises like Varuna (Navy), Garuda (Air Force), and Shakti (Army).
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution and significance of the India-France Strategic Partnership in the context of changing global geopolitics.
Discuss the key pillars of cooperation between India and France, particularly in defence, space, and nuclear energy.
Evaluate the role of India and France in promoting stability and security in the Indo-Pacific region.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for further deepening bilateral ties, including economic and technological collaboration.
Compare India's strategic relations with France to those with other major global powers.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and France displayed together during a diplomatic meeting.
GS2.trivia: The first Indian astronaut, Rakesh Sharma, flew aboard a Soviet spacecraft, Soyuz T-11, in 1984, which included a French cosmonaut.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Environmental Clearance for Key Infrastructure Project with Stricter Conditions
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court has upheld the environmental clearance granted to a major infrastructure project, but imposed stricter conditions for its implementation.
• The ruling emphasizes the judiciary's role in balancing economic development with environmental protection and sustainable practices.
• The project, critical for regional connectivity and economic growth, had faced challenges from environmental activists over potential ecological impact.
• The new conditions include enhanced compensatory afforestation, robust waste management plans, and regular environmental impact assessments.
• This judgment sets a precedent for future large-scale projects, mandating higher environmental accountability.
gsSubTopics: judiciary
government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) is mandatory for certain projects under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.
National Green Tribunal (NGT) deals with environmental cases.
Concept of 'polluter pays principle' and 'precautionary principle'.
Role of Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC).
mainsPoints: Discuss the intricate balance between economic development and environmental conservation in the context of India's growth trajectory.
Analyze the role of the judiciary, particularly the Supreme Court, in environmental governance and policy implementation.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) process in India and suggest necessary reforms.
Examine the concept of 'sustainable development' and how it is being integrated into infrastructure planning.
Discuss the challenges in ensuring compliance with environmental conditions for large-scale projects.
GS2.imageDescription: A highway passing through a green landscape, with a symbol of environmental protection superimposed.
GS2.trivia: India was the first country to make environmental protection a constitutional mandate (Article 48A and 51A(g)).

GS2.headline: Government Launches 'PM-SURAJ' Scheme for Skill Development in Rural Areas
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Government has officially launched the 'Pradhan Mantri – Samagra Unnayan aur Rozgar Abhiyan (PM-SURAJ)' scheme.
• The scheme aims to boost skill development and provide employment opportunities for rural youth and marginalized communities.
• It integrates existing skill development programs and introduces new initiatives focusing on demand-driven vocational training.
• PM-SURAJ emphasizes entrepreneurship development and access to credit for setting up small enterprises in rural India.
• The initiative is expected to play a crucial role in reducing rural unemployment and fostering inclusive growth.
gsSubTopics: government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Human Resources
prelimsPoints: PM-SURAJ is an umbrella scheme consolidating various skill development initiatives.
Focus on rural areas and vulnerable sections (SC, ST, OBC, EWS, sanitation workers).
Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment is likely the nodal ministry.
Other related schemes: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY), DDU-GKY.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of skill development in addressing rural unemployment and poverty in India.
Analyze the potential impact of the PM-SURAJ scheme on inclusive growth and social empowerment of marginalized communities.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing skill development programs in rural areas, including infrastructure, trainer availability, and market linkages.
Examine the role of public-private partnerships and digital platforms in enhancing the reach and effectiveness of such schemes.
Suggest measures to ensure that skill development initiatives are aligned with industry demands and future job market trends.
GS2.imageDescription: A group of young men and women learning vocational skills in a rural setting.
GS2.trivia: India has the world's largest youth population, presenting both a demographic dividend and a challenge for skill development.

GS2.headline: New Guidelines for Data Privacy in Healthcare Sector Released by Ministry of Health
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare has released comprehensive new guidelines aimed at strengthening data privacy and security in the healthcare sector.
• The guidelines address the collection, storage, sharing, and usage of sensitive personal health information (SPHI) by healthcare providers and digital platforms.
• They mandate strict consent mechanisms, data anonymization, and robust cybersecurity measures to protect patient confidentiality.
• The move comes in response to the increasing digitization of health records and concerns over potential data breaches and misuse.
• Experts commend the initiative as a crucial step towards building trust in digital health services and aligning with global data protection standards.
gsSubTopics: governance
issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health
e-governance
cyber security
GS2.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare / The Hindu BusinessLine
prelimsPoints: National Digital Health Mission (NDHM) aims to create a digital health ecosystem.
Personal Data Protection Bill, 2019 (expected to be enacted as law).
Right to Privacy recognized as a fundamental right (Puttaswamy judgment).
Role of Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) and Ministry of Health.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges and opportunities presented by the digitization of health records in India.
Analyze the importance of robust data privacy frameworks in building trust in digital health services and protecting patient rights.
Evaluate the key provisions of the new guidelines and their potential impact on healthcare providers and patients.
Examine the ethical considerations involved in handling sensitive personal health information.
Suggest measures to ensure effective implementation and enforcement of data privacy regulations in the healthcare sector.
GS2.imageDescription: A padlock icon over a medical record, symbolizing data security in healthcare.
GS2.trivia: The 'Aarogya Setu' app, launched during the COVID-19 pandemic, was one of India's largest digital health initiatives.

GS2.headline: Challenges in Urban Planning: Report Highlights Gaps in Smart Cities Mission Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• A recent report by an independent think tank highlights significant implementation gaps and challenges within India's Smart Cities Mission.
• The report points to issues such as inadequate public participation, funding bottlenecks, and lack of inter-agency coordination.
• While some cities have shown commendable progress, others struggle with project delays and failure to achieve desired outcomes in areas like urban mobility and waste management.
• The analysis suggests a need for re-evaluating the mission's strategy, focusing on capacity building at the municipal level and decentralized planning.
• Urban development experts advocate for a more inclusive and sustainable approach to smart city development.
gsSubTopics: government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
urbanization, their problems and their remedies
GS2.source: Centre for Policy Research / The Economic Times
prelimsPoints: Smart Cities Mission launched in 2015.
Aims to promote cities that provide core infrastructure, a clean and sustainable environment and application of 'Smart' solutions.
100 cities selected under the mission.
Other urban schemes: AMRUT, HRIDAY.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the performance and achievements of the Smart Cities Mission since its inception.
Discuss the major challenges in urban planning and governance that hinder the effective implementation of smart city projects.
Analyze the role of technology and citizen engagement in making urban areas more livable and sustainable.
Suggest policy reforms and strategic interventions to address the identified gaps in the Smart Cities Mission.
Examine the concept of 'inclusive urbanization' and how it can be integrated into future urban development plans.
GS2.imageDescription: An aerial view of a modern city skyline with digital overlays representing smart city concepts.
GS2.trivia: India's urban population is projected to reach 600 million by 2030, making urban planning a critical area.

GS2.headline: India Ratifies Key ILO Convention on Occupational Safety and Health
GS2.explainer:
• India has ratified a crucial International Labour Organization (ILO) Convention pertaining to occupational safety and health (OSH).
• This ratification signals India's commitment to ensuring safer working conditions and promoting the welfare of its vast workforce.
• The convention sets international standards for national policies and programs on OSH, including risk assessment, prevention, and training.
• The move is expected to drive reforms in India's labor laws and enforcement mechanisms to align with global best practices.
• Labor rights advocates welcome the ratification as a significant step towards protecting workers' rights and reducing workplace accidents.
gsSubTopics: welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
India and its neighborhood- relations
bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
GS2.source: Ministry of Labour & Employment / The Economic Times
prelimsPoints: ILO is a specialized agency of the United Nations.
India is a founding member of the ILO (1919).
Constitutional provisions related to labor: Concurrent List, Article 39(e), 42, 43.
Recent Labour Codes: Code on Wages, Industrial Relations Code, Code on Social Security, OSH & Working Conditions Code.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's ratification of the ILO Convention on Occupational Safety and Health for its workforce and international image.
Analyze the current state of occupational safety and health in India, highlighting major challenges across different sectors.
Evaluate the potential impact of this ratification on India's labor laws, enforcement mechanisms, and industrial practices.
Examine the role of trade unions, employers, and government in promoting a culture of safety at workplaces.
Suggest comprehensive measures to improve occupational safety and health standards, including training, technology, and regulatory oversight.
GS2.imageDescription: A pair of safety gloves and a hard hat on a workbench, symbolizing workplace safety.
GS2.trivia: The ILO was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize in 1969 for its efforts to improve peace among classes, pursue justice for workers, and provide technical assistance to developing nations.

GS3.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission: India Accelerates Towards Production Goals
GS3.explainer:
• India's ambitious National Green Hydrogen Mission is reportedly on track, with significant progress in policy framework and initial project allocations.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization.
• Recent policy announcements include incentives for electrolyser manufacturing and a framework for green hydrogen demand creation in key sectors like refining and fertilizers.
• This initiative is crucial for India to meet its net-zero emissions target by 2070 and reduce reliance on fossil fuel imports.
• Public-private partnerships are being encouraged to scale up production and reduce the cost of green hydrogen.
gsSubTopics: Green Hydrogen Mission
renewable energy
energy security
climate change mitigation
industrial growth
technological innovation
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy / Economic Times
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in 2023.
Target to produce 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030.
SIGHT (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) Programme is a key component.
Role of Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE).
Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the economic potential of green hydrogen as an export commodity and its impact on industrial sectors.
Examine the challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production, including technology costs, infrastructure, and storage.
Evaluate the role of government policies and international collaborations in achieving the mission's objectives.
Compare green hydrogen with other clean energy sources in the context of India's energy mix.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production facility with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Environment
prelimsSyllabus.area: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment

GS3.headline: Maharashtra Train Accident: 13 Killed, Railway Safety Under Intense Scrutiny
GS3.explainer:
• Thirteen people were killed and five others injured after a passenger train collided with a group of people near Pachora, Maharashtra.
• The victims had disembarked from another train due to a false fire alarm and were on the tracks when the collision occurred.
• The incident has raised serious questions about railway safety protocols, crowd management, and emergency response mechanisms.
• Railway authorities have initiated a high-level inquiry to determine the cause and accountability for the tragic loss of life.
• Experts are calling for enhanced public awareness campaigns regarding railway safety and stricter enforcement of rules.
gsSubTopics: disaster and disaster management
infrastructure (railways)
internal security challenges
prelimsPoints: Indian Railways is the fourth-largest railway network in the world.
Railways Act, 1989 governs railway operations in India.
Role of Commissioner of Railway Safety (CRS) for accident inquiries.
Kavach system (Automatic Train Protection) is being implemented to prevent collisions.
mainsPoints: Analyze the systemic issues contributing to railway accidents in India, including infrastructure, human error, and safety protocols.
Discuss the measures that can be adopted to enhance railway safety, focusing on technology, public awareness, and emergency response.
Evaluate the challenges in managing large crowds at railway stations and during emergencies.
Examine the role of regulatory bodies and government policies in ensuring safe railway operations.
Suggest ways to improve coordination between different agencies during railway accidents and disaster relief operations.
GS3.imageDescription: Rescue workers near train tracks after an accident, with emergency vehicles.
GS3.trivia: The first passenger train in India ran on April 16, 1853, from Bori Bunder (Mumbai) to Thane.

GS3.headline: RBI Releases Quarterly Inflation Report: Focus on Food Prices and Global Volatility
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has released its latest quarterly inflation report, highlighting persistent concerns over food price inflation.
• The report indicates that global supply chain disruptions and geopolitical tensions continue to contribute to inflationary pressures.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to maintaining price stability while supporting economic growth, signaling a cautious approach to monetary policy.
• Analysts suggest that the central bank might hold interest rates steady in the near term, awaiting clearer trends in inflation and economic activity.
• The report also emphasizes the need for structural reforms to address supply-side bottlenecks and improve agricultural productivity.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment
inflation
monetary policy
prelimsPoints: RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) sets the policy interest rate (repo rate) to achieve the inflation target.
Inflation target for CPI is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
Tools of monetary policy include repo rate, reverse repo rate, CRR, SLR, OMOs.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to current inflationary trends in India, distinguishing between demand-pull and cost-push inflation.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with economic growth objectives.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in managing inflation in the Indian context.
Examine the impact of global economic volatility and geopolitical events on India's inflation outlook.
Suggest fiscal and supply-side measures that the government can implement to complement RBI's efforts in managing inflation.
GS3.imageDescription: A chart showing inflation trends with upward arrows, overlaid with the RBI logo.
GS3.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology Demonstrator
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has successfully conducted a critical test flight of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator.
• This achievement marks a significant step towards developing a fully reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle, reducing launch costs.
• The test involved a controlled landing experiment, simulating the return of a space vehicle to Earth.
• Reusable launch vehicles are crucial for future space exploration and commercial space missions, offering economic and environmental benefits.
• ISRO aims to master this technology to enhance India's competitiveness in the global space market.
gsSubTopics: achievements of Indians in science & technology
indigenization of technology and developing new technology
space technology
prelimsPoints: RLV-TD is India's first step towards developing a reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle.
It aims to reduce the cost of space access significantly.
Key experiments include Hypersonic Flight Experiment (HEX), Landing Experiment (LEX), Return Flight Experiment (REX), Scramjet Propulsion Experiment (SPEX).
ISRO is headquartered in Bengaluru.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's space program and its global standing.
Analyze the economic and environmental benefits of developing reusable launch vehicles.
Examine the challenges involved in mastering RLV technology, including materials science, aerodynamics, and propulsion systems.
Evaluate ISRO's trajectory in space technology development and its contribution to 'Atmanirbhar Bharat'.
Suggest how India can leverage RLV technology to further commercialize its space sector.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) descending for a landing.
GS3.trivia: ISRO holds the record for launching 104 satellites in a single mission in February 2017, using the PSLV-C37.

GS3.headline: India's Renewable Energy Capacity Crosses 200 GW Mark: A Milestone Achievement
GS3.explainer:
• India has achieved a significant milestone, with its installed renewable energy capacity (excluding large hydro) now exceeding 200 Gigawatts (GW).
• This achievement underscores India's rapid progress in its energy transition and commitment to climate goals.
• Solar and wind power constitute the largest share of this capacity, driven by favorable government policies and decreasing technology costs.
• The milestone brings India closer to its target of 500 GW non-fossil fuel capacity by 2030.
• This expansion is crucial for enhancing energy security, reducing carbon emissions, and attracting green investments.
gsSubTopics: conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
infrastructure: energy
science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: India's target: 500 GW non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
International Solar Alliance (ISA) is an Indian initiative.
Government schemes: PM-KUSUM, Solar Parks Scheme.
Major renewable energy sources: Solar, Wind, Biomass, Small Hydro.
mainsPoints: Discuss the factors that have contributed to India's rapid growth in renewable energy capacity.
Analyze the economic and environmental benefits of transitioning to a renewable energy-dominated power sector.
Examine the challenges in integrating high shares of intermittent renewable energy into the national grid.
Evaluate the government's policies and initiatives aimed at promoting renewable energy, including financial incentives and regulatory frameworks.
Suggest future strategies for India to achieve its ambitious renewable energy targets and ensure energy security.
GS3.imageDescription: A vast solar power plant stretching to the horizon under a clear sky.
GS3.trivia: The world's largest solar park, Bhadla Solar Park, is located in Rajasthan, India.

GS3.headline: Border Security Force Foils Major Infiltration Bid in Jammu Sector
GS3.explainer:
• The Border Security Force (BSF) successfully foiled a major infiltration bid along the International Border in the Jammu sector.
• BSF troops detected and engaged a group of armed infiltrators attempting to cross into Indian territory under the cover of dense fog.
• The incident highlights the persistent security challenges faced by India along its western borders, particularly from state-sponsored terrorism.
• Security forces remain on high alert, intensifying surveillance and patrolling activities to counter such threats.
• This operation underscores the unwavering vigilance and professionalism of India's border guarding forces.
gsSubTopics: security challenges and their management in border areas
linkages of organized crime with terrorism
various security forces and agencies and their mandate
GS3.source: Ministry of Home Affairs / The Economic Times
prelimsPoints: BSF is India's primary border guarding organization on the Pakistan and Bangladesh borders.
It comes under the Ministry of Home Affairs.
Line of Control (LoC) vs International Border (IB).
Role of various Central Armed Police Forces (CAPFs).
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolving nature of cross-border infiltration and its implications for India's internal security.
Discuss the multi-pronged strategy employed by Indian security forces to counter infiltration and terrorism in border areas.
Evaluate the role of technology, such as surveillance systems and anti-infiltration obstacle systems, in enhancing border security.
Examine the geopolitical factors contributing to persistent border challenges, particularly in the Jammu and Kashmir region.
Suggest measures to strengthen coordination between various security agencies for effective border management.
GS3.imageDescription: BSF jawans patrolling a foggy border area with weapons.
GS3.trivia: The BSF was formed in 1965 in the wake of the 1965 Indo-Pak War.

sources: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy / Economic Times
Ministry of External Affairs / The Indian Express
Ministry of Health and Family Welfare / The Hindu BusinessLine
Centre for Policy Research / The Economic Times
Archaeological Survey of India / The Economic Times
